articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Colombia stun Germany 2-0 in latest setback for Euro 2024 hosts
article.description: Germany suffered the first loss in their history against Colombia in a friendly on Tuesday in their latest setback ahead of the Euro 2024.
article.linkText: Colombia extend Germany winless streak to 4 games

images.name: Germany woe vs Colombia_230620 [1296x729]
images.caption: Germany have won only one of five games since a second straight group-stage exit in Qatar 2022.
images.alt: Germany players react after a friendly loss to Colombia in Gelsenkirchen.

article.story:
Germany suffered a 2-0 home loss to Colombia in a friendly on Tuesday in their latest setback less than a year before they host Euro 2024.

Luis Diaz's 54th-minute header and Juan Cuadrado's penalty in the 82nd gave the visitors their first victory over the Germans, who were booed at the end.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

The Germans, who have less than a year to build a battle-hardened team for the continental tournament, had needed two late goals to draw 3-3 with Ukraine last week before Friday's 1-0 loss to Poland.

The four-time world champions have won just one of their last five matches since their shock World Cup group-stage exit in December last year. They have also won just three of their last 11 matches.

"Obviously I am very disappointed that we could not apply what we had set out to do," said Germany coach Hansi Flick.

"There are things that we wanted to try but they backfired."

Flick, who took over in 2021 and is under mounting pressure since the early World Cup elimination, said his players were lacking in confidence.

"What can I say? The arguments are not on our side at the moment. We have to analyse it, draw our lessons," he said after three games without a win.

"This is a cycle that we need to break through. In September we have to bring a different performance. We will see a different team and then the results will start coming," he said. "We are convinced we have a good team and good players."

The Colombians looked far hungrier in attack, forcing Germany keeper Marc Andre ter Stegen to make a string of good saves in the first half, while Germany's 21-year-old Malick Thiaw was impressive in defence.

The hosts had more than 65% possession but did not manage a single shot on goal for the entire first half, often struggling to play the ball out of their own half.

Colombia took the lead after Germany's Emre Can lost possession and Cuadrado whipped in a cross for Diaz to head past Ter Stegen.

Germany briefly upped the tempo but were still vulnerable at the back.

The introduction of centre forward Niclas Fuellkrug did little to improve things up front and a Joshua Kimmich handball gave the visitors a chance to double their lead with Cuadrado scoring from the spot.
